pi_items.source: Verified against export_result.sh which copies this exact path from the wazuh manager container.
Verified against export_result.sh which explicitly checks for this file path on the host.

Verified against the task description prompt and the explicit hardcoded checks in verifier.py. I also confirmed via web search that the default Wazuh rules referenced (5710 for SSH non-existent user auth attempt, 5715 for SSH authentication success, and 5902 for new user added) are correct and exist in the standard Wazuh rulesets.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates entirely on synthetically created custom rules. I confirmed the target Wazuh rules file path `/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml` matches the standard configuration and the extraction path in export_result.sh. The expected verification file path `/home/ga/correlated_rules_verification.json` exactly matches export_result.sh. The required custom rules (100300, 100301, 100302) are correctly outlined in the metadata, mapping to exact criteria in the task description and verifier.py. The base rules referenced in the criteria (5710 for failed SSH login on non-existent user, 5715 for successful SSH login, and 5902 for a new user creation) are valid, default Wazuh rules.
